title: Tablet VI — Ishtar and the Bull of Heaven
source: CDLI tablet photograph (P273210) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
description: Tablet VI — Ishtar and the Bull of Heaven of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Ishtar's proposal to Gilgamesh; his rejection; the Bull of Heaven; death of the Bull. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P273210). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).
introduction:
This entry presents Tablet VI — Ishtar and the Bull of Heaven of the Epic of Gilgamesh — the oldest major work of literature, composed in Akkadian cuneiform around 2100-1200 BCE.

  1. Tablet photograph: Cuneiform tablet photo from CDLI (artifact P273210), showing the actual clay tablet with the wedge-shaped script.
  2. Original text: Akkadian transliteration from Langdon's scholarly edition (public domain).
  3. English translation: Langdon's English rendering (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

translation.original_ref: Tablet VI — Ishtar and the Bull of Heaven, section 1
translation.original_text:
Gunura, goddess of healing, 176, 6.

_hanabu_, grow thickly, Prs. _ibannib_, 219, 4.

_harbatu_, waste place, 200, 39.

translation.original_text:
Igigi, heaven spirits, 116 n. 6.

_imib_, weapon, 131, 8. _mi-ib_, ibid. n.3.

_imin_, seven. Seven lands, 130, 35; seventh day, 134, 18.

translation.original_text:
Indag, god, consort of Gula, 173, 3.
